evals.prompt:
I need a bar complaint against Wyatt S. Baum, OSB #111773, Baum Smith LLC, 808 Adams Avenue, La Grande, OR 97850. Case is In re Guardianship/Conservatorship of Russell Bingaman, Union County Circuit Court, Case No. 23PR02271. Prior CAO Ref DPA 2500773 dismissed May 2025.

Here's what happened:
1. Baum coordinated with guardians to lock Russell's wife Patty out of visits using an IBL form even though DHS told them in Oct 2023 that IBLs can't be used to restrict spousal access. The AFH owner testified she locked the doors based on 'licensing permission' from the guardians, not a court order.
2. Darren Murchison (spouse of guardian Cheryl, who has no court appointment) paid Baum's fees on 7/8/2024 and Cheryl signed the IBL and participated in strategy calls. No ORPC 1.8(f) consent on file.
3. Baum billed the family $13,670.94 privately mid-2024, then sought $13,934.73 from the estate in March 2025 for the same period, plus a supplemental $3,048.50.
4. Initial inventory showed $28,386 in BEO account ...0497. Amended inventory after Russell's death on 1/29/2025 showed $35,500.61 in BEO ...3812. That's a $7,114.61 swing with no explanation.
5. Baum had hospice records by 7/24/2024 showing a 'six-month' prognosis was used to enroll Russell. By 12/20/2024 Baum wrote that providers 'placed the six-month predicate to enable hospice.' His clients didn't know about a qualifying diagnosis.
6. On 9/4/2024, a 'clean' PDF set was created with no producer/app metadata and received 7 minutes later — this was 6 days before the hearing. Baum had the actual prognosis PDF since at least 7/24/2024 (created 2:47 PM) and it was modified 8/2/2024 on a ScanSnap scanner.
7. In January 2025, Baum proposed lifting visitation restrictions only if Patty withdrew her Petition for Appointment of a Third-Party Guardian. Visitation is governed by ORS 125.323, not a bargaining chip.

Exhibits: A = DHS/APS email 10/26/2023, B = June 25 2024 eviction threat email, C = Jan 7-8 2025 settlement emails, D = May 8 2024 property purchase email, E = Dec 20 2024 six-month predicate email, F = billing ledger excerpts, G = amended petition for fees, H = initial vs amended inventory, I = corrective packet served 2/24/2025, J = guardians log 7/10/2024, K = hearing transcript 9/10/2024, L = financial records showing Darren Murchison payment, M = IBL form APD-0556, N-1 through N-11 = PDF metadata and production documents, O = March 2024 Family Agreement.

Complainant: Levi M. Bakke, 501 C Ave, La Grande OR 97850, (971) 303-4982, MRDcontractor@gmail.com
evals.expected_output: A well-structured .docx bar complaint with 7-8 counts, each tied to specific ORPC rules, with exhibit references and referral rationales. Should match the structure and tone of the August 29 Baum complaint.

evals.prompt:
Draft a bar complaint. Attorney is Sandra K. Mitchell, OSB #098541, Mitchell & Associates, 1200 NE Broadway, Portland OR 97232. Phone 503-555-0142, email smitchell@mitchelllaw.com.

Case: Thornton v. Thornton, Multnomah County Circuit Court, Case No. 24DR08891 (divorce).

My name is David Thornton, 4521 SE Hawthorne Blvd, Portland OR 97215, phone 503-555-7823, email dthornton@gmail.com.

Mitchell represents my ex-wife. During discovery she produced bank statements but redacted a $45,000 transfer to her client's brother that I found independently through my own bank's records. When I raised it, she told the court the transfer was 'not relevant to marital assets' even though it happened 3 weeks before the petition was filed. She also billed my ex $18,000 for a 4-month period but I obtained her fee agreement which caps at $350/hr and her billing shows only 38 hours. That's $13,300 at most. She's also representing my ex in the divorce while simultaneously representing my ex's brother in a real estate transaction involving property that's part of the marital estate.

I have: the redacted vs unredacted bank statements, my own bank records showing the transfer, the court transcript where she called it 'not relevant', her fee agreement, billing statements showing 38 hours, and the retainer agreement with the brother showing the same property address.
evals.expected_output: A .docx bar complaint with 3-4 counts covering discovery misconduct/candor, fee issues, and conflict of interest, with appropriate ORPC citations.

evals.prompt: bar complaint against my lawyer who ghosted me. James Rivera OSB 145290. He took my $5000 retainer for a custody case in Deschutes County (Case 24FL01234) back in March 2024, filed the initial petition, then stopped returning my calls and emails starting June 2024. My hearing was in September and he didn't show up. The judge continued it but I had to find a new lawyer. He never refunded any of the retainer and his office voicemail is full. I've sent 3 certified letters. I'm Sarah Chen, 61000 Brookswood Blvd, Bend OR 97702, 541-555-3847, schen.bend@gmail.com. His office was at 845 NW Wall St Suite 200, Bend OR 97701, phone 541-555-9021, jrivera@riveralaw.net.
evals.expected_output: A .docx bar complaint with 2-3 counts covering abandonment/diligence (1.3, 1.4), failure to communicate (1.4), and safekeeping/accounting of retainer (1.15, 1.16). Should maintain professional tone despite the casual input.
